summary: STORY-443 is a documentation refactor with no production code, no APIs, no databases, no external services. Only cross-component interaction is dual-path synchronization (src/ vs .claude/ copies). Existing unit tests comprehensively cover all integration points. No additional integration tests required.

observations.note: STORY-443 classified as documentation refactor (type: refactor) in YAML frontmatter line 4. No production code changes, no API endpoints, no database operations, no external service integrations.

Anti-gaming validation PASSED all 6 checks: (0.1) 0 skip decorators, (0.2) 0 empty tests, (0.3) 0 TODO/FIXME placeholders, (0.4) 0 mocks vs 76 tests (ratio 0:76 acceptable), (0.5/0.6) all checks pass.

Existing test suite includes 76 passing unit tests: AC#1(11), AC#2(6), AC#3(8), AC#4(15), AC#5(15), AC#6(17). All tests use AAA pattern (Arrange, Act, Assert) with explicit assertions. No mocking or test doubles used. Pure content verification and grep-based validation.

Only cross-component interaction identified: AC#6 tests dual-path synchronization between src/claude/memory/skills-reference.md and .claude/memory/skills-reference.md (17 tests). This is the only file-to-file interaction.

AC#6 dual-path sync tests comprehensively cover: old invocations absent from both copies (6 tests), new invocations present in both copies (6 tests), section presence consistent (3 tests), old name absence synchronized (2 tests). Full integration coverage provided.
No API contracts to validate. STORY-443 contains no API endpoints. AC#1-5 are file content and grep-sweep tests. AC#6 is file-to-file synchronization. No API schema validation needed.
No database operations in STORY-443. All changes are file-based documentation updates and verification sweeps. No database transactions to test, no commit/rollback scenarios.
No external service integrations in STORY-443. All operations are local file system operations (read, grep, write). No HTTP calls, no API clients, no external dependencies requiring mocks.

AC#5 codebase sweep acts as E2E validation: validates entire codebase for zero stale references across all active directories. Comprehensive end-to-end verification of migration completeness across all touched components.
All 76 tests PASSED in 9.49 seconds on Feb 18 15:37 UTC. No failures, no warnings, no skipped tests. Full test suite execution successful.

NO ADDITIONAL INTEGRATION TESTS REQUIRED. Rationale: (1) Story type is documentation-only (refactor), not production code; (2) Only cross-component interaction is AC#6 dual-path sync, already covered by 17 existing tests; (3) No APIs, databases, or external services to integrate; (4) AC#5 codebase sweep validates end-to-end migration success; (5) Anti-gaming validation confirms test authenticity.

recommendation: STORY-443 requires ZERO additional integration tests. The existing 76 unit tests comprehensively cover all requirements. AC#6 (dual-path synchronization) provides complete cross-component validation. AC#5 (codebase sweep) provides E2E verification. All tests are authentic (no gaming patterns detected). Proceed directly to QA phase.
next_steps: Proceed to QA phase with existing 76 tests
Run AC#6 tests to validate dual-path sync before release
Run AC#5 sweep to confirm zero stale references in codebase
